title: Preparation of Recording ACSF (artificial cerebrospinal fluid) for slice electrophysiology
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b0tp6","text":"Hnasko lab- preparation of Recording ACSF","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3hkdc","text":"Recording ACSF is used for incubating brain slices during cell-attached or whole-cell electrophysiological recordings of neurons.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"3nrnm","text":"NaCl (Fisher Scientific, cat# BP358)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fmtvs","text":"KCl (Fisher Scientific, cat# BP366)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a9mrb","text":"NaH2PO4 (Fisher Scientific, cat# BP329)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":3,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":6,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3g40c","text":"D-glucose (Sigma Aldrich, cat# G8270)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"60aij","text":"CaCl2 (Fisher Scientific, cat# BP510)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":4,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"be97r","text":"MgSO4  (Fisher Scientific, cat# M80)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":4,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7mtid","text":"NaHCO3 (Fisher Scientific, cat# BP328)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":5,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7nb83","text":"HCl (Fisher Scientific, cat# A481-212)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Composition of 1 L of 10X Recording ACSF (in mM): 1250 NaCl, 25 KCl, 12 NaH 2 PO 4 , 125 d-glucose, 20 CaCl 2 and 20 MgSO 4 .
Composition of 1 L of 10X Recording ACSF (in g): 73.05 NaCl, 1.86 KCl, 1.44 NaH 2 PO 4 , 22.52 d-glucose, 2.94 CaCl 2 and 4.93 MgSO 4 .
Dissolve all salts in 1 L of ddH 2 O to make 10X stock solution of Recording ACSF.
Dilute 10X solution to 1X for use in experiments.
Dilute 100 mL of 10X solution to ~ 800 mL with ddH 2 O and add (in mM): 26 NaHCO 3 ; (in g): 2.18 NaHCO 3 .
Add ddH 2 O to final volume of 1 L .
Bubble the solution with carbogen (95% O 2 5% CO 2 ) for ~10 min.
After bubbling, measure and adjust pH of solution to ~ pH 7.3 with 12 N HCl (<1 ml).
creator: Sarthak M. Singhal
